name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: Title lender offering car and motorcycle title loans up to $10,000 with no credit check required; funds available in as little as 30 minutes at 1,100+ stores across 16 states.
description_long:
TitleMax was founded on September 1, 1998, opening its first location in Columbus, Georgia. The company operates as TMX Finance LLC d/b/a TitleMax and is now part of the Community Choice Financial (CCF) family of brands. With over 1,100 stores across 16 states, it is one of the largest title lending chains in the United States. TitleMax holds no CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications — it is a for-profit, privately held title lender, not a nonprofit or community development institution. Corporate offices are based in Savannah, Georgia and Dallas, Texas.

TitleMax's primary product is the car title loan: a short-term loan secured by the borrower's vehicle title, with amounts up to $10,000. Motorcycle title loans follow the same structure. In Georgia stores, the product is offered as a title pawn, which is functionally similar. TitleMax also offers unsecured personal loans up to $2,500 for customers with verifiable income. No credit check is required for title loan approval — eligibility is based on vehicle value and income. The borrower retains possession of and continues driving the vehicle during the loan term. Applications can be submitted online via the Fast Track Appraiser app or in-store, with funding available in as little as 30 minutes. No bank account is required to qualify.

TitleMax's core differentiators are speed, accessibility, and footprint. Same-day funding in under 30 minutes sets it apart from traditional lenders. Its no-credit-check model opens the door for consumers who are shut out of banks and credit unions. The 1,100+ store network means in-person service is available in most markets it serves. The ability to keep driving your vehicle — unlike a traditional pawn where you surrender the collateral — is a practical advantage over pawnshop-style lending.

However, TitleMax carries serious financial and regulatory risks that consumers must weigh carefully. APR is not publicly disclosed on their website, but industry-standard rates for title loans far exceed 300% annually, and monthly fees can reach approximately 25% of the borrowed amount depending on state. The company has a significant regulatory history: in 2016, the CFPB cited TitleMax for misrepresenting loan costs and deceptive sales practices. In 2023, the CFPB ordered TitleMax to pay $15 million — $5 million in consumer redress and a $10 million civil penalty — for violating the Military Lending Act by charging military families nearly three times the 36% interest rate cap and altering borrower records to conceal it. TitleMax is not BBB accredited, and multiple BBB profiles carry complaints related to loan disputes, vehicle repossession, and identity theft claims. Borrowers who cannot repay risk losing their vehicle entirely.
address: 2720 N Belt Line Rd Ste 100, Irving, TX 75062

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. This is a loan product — not applicable.

pros: No credit check required — loan approval based on vehicle value and income, not credit score
Funds available in as little as 30 minutes — among the fastest title loan turnarounds available
Borrower keeps and drives their vehicle throughout the loan term
No bank account required to qualify for a title loan
1,100+ store locations across 16 states plus online application option
Founded in 1998 — over 25 years of title lending operations
Fast Track Appraiser app enables remote vehicle appraisal for online applications
cons: APR not disclosed publicly; industry-typical title loan rates exceed 300% APR annually
2023 CFPB enforcement action resulted in $15 million penalty for violating the Military Lending Act — including altering borrower records
Vehicle repossession is a real consequence of default — borrowers risk losing their primary transportation
Not BBB accredited; multiple BBB profiles carry complaints about repossession, loan disputes, and identity theft
Monthly fees can reach approximately 25% of the borrowed amount depending on state law
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit history who own a paid-off vehicle outright
People needing same-day cash access who cannot qualify for a bank loan or credit card
Borrowers who do not have a bank account and need a lender with no banking requirement
Individuals needing between $500 and $10,000 secured against their vehicle title
diagnosis: TitleMax is best suited for consumers with no viable credit options who own a vehicle free and clear and need fast cash — it delivers on speed and accessibility where traditional lenders cannot. The critical caveat is cost and risk: interest rates routinely exceed 300% APR, default leads to vehicle repossession, and TitleMax has faced major CFPB enforcement actions including a 2023 penalty for predatory lending targeting military families. This is a last-resort product, not a routine borrowing tool.
services: Fast Track Appraiser smartphone app for remote vehicle appraisal
Same-day funding in as little as 30 minutes
Customer online portal for account and payment management
Vehicle title lien processing and release
